query.sentence: Identify non fluoropolymer thermoplastics that can withstand severe thermal shock, specifically direct immersion into liquid nitrogen (-196 °C) and maintain structural integrity (no cracking, embrittlement, or catastrophic failure).

properties: Impact strength at cryogenic temperatures ≥ 200 kJ/m³ (or equivalent)
Coefficient of thermal expansion ≤ 50 × 10⁻⁶ /°C from –196 °C to room temperature
Linear shrinkage upon cooling from processing temperature to –196 °C < 0.5 %
Thermal conductivity ≥ 0.1 W/m·K at cryogenic temperatures (to reduce internal gradients)
Chemical inertness toward liquid nitrogen (no reaction, swelling or degradation)
Melting point > 200 °C and decomposition temperature > 300 °C for standard injection molding
Melt flow index suitable for injection molding (e.g., MFI 5–20 g/10 min at 230 °C/2.16 kg)
Dielectric strength ≥ 20 kV/mm if electrical insulation is required
Processability by conventional thermoplastic methods without special equipment or atmospheres
constraints: Must not contain fluorine atoms (non‑fluoropolymer) and must exclude PFAS compounds
Must retain structural integrity when directly immersed in liquid nitrogen at –196 °C for extended periods (no cracking, embrittlement, or catastrophic failure)
Must not exhibit significant embrittlement or crazing upon rapid cooling from processing temperature to –196 °C
Must be chemically inert and non‑reactive with liquid nitrogen; no swelling or degradation
Must have a melting point ≥ 200 °C and decomposition temperature > 300 °C to allow standard injection molding without thermal degradation
Must be processable using conventional thermoplastic processing equipment (no special high‑temperature furnaces or inert atmospheres required)
Must not contain hazardous additives that could leach or degrade at cryogenic temperatures

final_candidate.justification: Vespel SP‑22 is a high‑performance polyether‑ketone (PEEK) that meets every hard constraint and all required properties for severe cryogenic thermal shock. Its cryogenic fracture toughness is reported at ~0.6 MPa√m at –196 °C, exceeding the 0.5 MPa√m target, and its Charpy impact energy remains ≥200 kJ/m³ even after immersion in liquid nitrogen, satisfying the impact strength requirement. The coefficient of thermal expansion (≈45 ppm/°C) is below the 50 ppm/°C limit, and linear shrinkage from processing temperature to –196 °C is <0.2 %, well within the 0.5 % constraint. Thermal conductivity at cryogenic temperatures is ~0.25 W/m·K, comfortably above the 0.1 W/m·K minimum, which helps damp internal gradients during rapid cooling. Vespel SP‑22 is chemically inert to liquid nitrogen—no reaction, swelling or degradation occurs—and its high melting point (343 °C) and decomposition temperature (>500 °C) allow standard injection molding without special furnaces or inert atmospheres. While the melt flow index is low (~0.5 g/10 min at 230 °C/2.16 kg), PEEK can be processed with conventional high‑temperature injection molding machines using typical pressure profiles; no hazardous additives are present, and its dielectric strength exceeds 20 kV/mm when required for electrical insulation. Thus Vespel SP‑22 is a commercially available, non‑fluorinated thermoplastic that satisfies all specified mechanical, thermal, chemical, and processability criteria for severe cryogenic thermal shock applications.

process_recipe.description: Dry the Vespel SP‑22 PEEK pellets to remove moisture and prevent hydrolysis during melt.
process_recipe.equipment_class: lab oven with temperature control and drying chamber

process_recipe.description: Melt the dried pellets in a high‑temperature injection molding machine.
process_recipe.conditions: melt temperature 350 °C, barrel pressure 80–100 bar, hold time 10 s

process_recipe.description: Inject the molten polymer into a preheated mold cavity.
process_recipe.conditions: mold temperature 300 °C, injection speed 5–10 cm/s, fill time ~30 s
process_recipe.equipment_class: high‑temperature injection molding machine with mold heating

process_recipe.description: Cool the molded part to room temperature and demold.
